action.hypothesis: The `LR=1.6e-3` exploitation probe at `K=96` clearly underperformed, so `3.2e-3` should remain the routed-family baseline and the next clean exploitation dimension is auxiliary dead-feature pressure. The specific hypothesis is that a small nonzero `AUXK_ALPHA=0.01` can improve feature utilization for `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` at the valuable `K=96` anchor without the severe optimization regression seen from lowering LR.
action.summary: Run one parameter-only exploitation experiment at `K=96` with `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `LR=3.2e-3` fixed while changing only `AUXK_ALPHA` from `0.0` to `0.01`. Success criterion: beat the current `K=96` frontier anchor FVU (`0.06254687905311584`) and determine whether light aux-k pressure improves the routed family on the strongest smaller-K high-quality tradeoff point.
action.self_review: This follows the exploitation policy and changes exactly one parameter after the lower-LR test failed badly. I kept the target at `K=96` because it remains the more valuable Pareto anchor than `K=128` if quality improves. The main risk is that aux pressure may disturb the routed recipe the same way it hurt the plain soft-codebook family, but this is the cleanest remaining exploitation branch.
notes_to_memory: Round 102 showed `LR=1.6e-3` is a clear regression for `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` at `K=96`, so `LR=3.2e-3` should remain the routed-family baseline.
Round 103 should switch exploitation dimensions from learning rate to aux-k pressure while keeping the valuable `K=96` routed anchor fixed.
Keep all recipe fields fixed except `AUXK_ALPHA`; this isolates whether light dead-feature pressure improves the current best routed family without confounding with another LR change.
next_hypotheses: If `AUXK_ALPHA=0.01` wins at `K=96`, propagate the same aux setting to `K=128` under the fixed routed recipe to test whether the gain generalizes to the top-quality anchor.
If it is close but still behind at `K=96`, test the stronger `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625` before abandoning aux tuning on the routed family.
If it clearly loses at `K=96`, keep `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` as the routed baseline and use the next exploitation round for the remaining routed LR values only if policy still requires exhausting them.

result.description: Run one parameter-only exploitation experiment at `K=96` with `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `LR=3.2e-3` fixed while changing only `AUXK_ALPHA` from `0.0` to `0.01`. Success criterion: beat the current `K=96` frontier anchor FVU (`0.06254687905311584`) and determine whether light aux-k pressure improves the routed family on the strongest smaller-K high-quality tradeoff point.
result.self_review: This follows the exploitation policy and changes exactly one parameter after the lower-LR test failed badly. I kept the target at `K=96` because it remains the more valuable Pareto anchor than `K=128` if quality improves. The main risk is that aux pressure may disturb the routed recipe the same way it hurt the plain soft-codebook family, but this is the cleanest remaining exploitation branch.
